reason:
Both answers correctly cover the core §412 grace-period rule, note the window closed around mid-April, correctly state registration is required to sue at all (Fourth Estate), and recommend registering now plus DMCA takedown as a stopgap — all key points are hit by both.

The differentiator is the nuance about ongoing/continuing infringement. Answer B flatly asserts "courts treat ongoing infringement as commencing at the first act — no reset," presenting this as settled law, then somewhat undercuts itself by suggesting the user "pin down when the infringement began" as if new infringement after registration could still count — which is confusing given the "no reset" claim it just made. Answer A instead accurately flags that this is a genuinely split area among courts (single-act vs. series-of-acts theories for continuing infringement), which is a more legally precise characterization and better calibrates the user's expectations without overstating certainty either direction.

Both are similar in length, clarity, and actionability, so the deciding factor is Answer A's slightly more accurate and consistent treatment of the continuing-infringement nuance.
